cpu: AMD A8-6500 APU with Radeon HD Graphics
notes: In order to run the game at all, you've got to run "protontricks 729040 dotnet40" - otherwise it'll die immediately after you try to launch.  Once you've done that, it starts out pretty promisingly, but the main problem is there's very severe video glitching as, presumably, data is being loaded - as if there's a video slider and it's going back and forth constantly, so animations are jerking back and forth.  On the main menu and the couple of intro cinematics, this just lasts for a couple seconds for me, but once I get to Claptrap handing me the HUD, it doesn't stop, and within a few tens of seconds the process seems to just lock up, sometimes with some crazy graphical glitches.  Performance otherwise seems quite promising, actually, but at the moment it's unplayable for me.  Disabling Esync makes performance MUCH worse, so don't bother trying that, and the game requires DX11.

notes:
Game runs very smoothly, ran with everything else on max, except for shadows because I hate them. FPS was high at all times. Played around for ~40 minutes and had no issues with anything.

Won't work out of the box though, as the launcher seems to never start. Workaround to this: Open game directory, (Right click game and select "Properties," go to "Local Files" and click "Browse Local Files...") open "Binaries/Win64", rename/delete "Launcher.exe" and rename "BorderlandGOTY.exe" to "Launcher.exe"
title: Borderlands Game of the Year Enhanced

notes:
In the game folder swap Launcher.exe and BorderlandsGOTY.exe (change Launcher.exe to Launcher.exe.old, change BorderlandsGOTY.exe to Launcher.exe, change Launcher.exe.old to BorlandsGOTY.exe.). Then force the game to run with Proton 4.2. Next, in the game's video settings change the FPS lock to unlimited.

https://www.reddit.com/r/linux_gaming/comments/b956mj/borderlands_goty_enhanced_runs_on_linux_via/

notes: This is a common tweak for games with launches (i.e. Sonic Adventure 2). Performance-wise, the game is perfect. I ran on max settings. Consistent 60fps if you set the framelock, about 120fps+ if you unlock it. I personally keep it at 60fps locked. You need to rename "Launcher.exe" to anything else, and rename "BorderlandsGOTY.exe" to "Launcher.exe." Even if you set Skip Launcher in the in-game settings, you still need to do this. I haven't tested performance on Windows but with the renaming the game is buttery smooth and I almost forgot I was playing with Proton.

title: Borderlands Game of the Year Enhanced

notes:
First some notes on how to get it working. As discussed on the Game's Github issue page, the game does not launch for some. The fix that worked for me was to go into the steamapps/coomon/BorderlandsGOTYEnhanced/Binaries/Win64 folder. I renamed 'Launcher.exe' to 'Launcher_back.exe' and renamed 'BorderlandsGOTY.exe' to 'Launcher.exe'. This allows the game to run and fixes the issue in the logs of Wine-Mono not being present.

Then by default it was a little choppy, but all I had to was turn the framerate to "Unlimited" in the settings and it ran smoothly.

I played all the begging mission in Fyrestone and loaded in The Dahl Headlands (the second area/level). There were no issues at all and the game played fine at 2560x1440. Will update if I experience any other issues.
title: Borderlands Game of the Year Enhanced

notes:
Tested single player up to just after clap-trap hands you the HUD. I was able to start the game after deleting the Launcher.exe and renaming a copy of BorderlandsGOTY.exe to Launcher.exe (otherwise the game won't launch).

The game experienced heavy stuttering when i got into the menu and eventually freezes. After I managed to set the Framerate cap to unlimited using the in-game settings, this stuttering and freezing stopped. Even at high settings on 1440p and with the uncapped framerate I experience no more stuttering or freezing and the game plays great.
title: Borderlands Game of the Year Enhanced

Disable V-sync and set Framerate to Unlimited in Video settings. If using Nvidia GPUs and there's black square artifacting, turn off Depth of Field & Bloom in Video settings.

notes:
had to do the launcher rename thing. beyond that, cranked the settings to max (1080p), I'll note the fps did not lock like a i expected at a firm 60fps. although i saw improvement once i changed it to a locked 75fps (my screen is only 60hz), most of the issue disappeared. although i'm a little iffy and giving it a gold, i think i'm simply a nudge below "maximum" in reality, rather then the game at fault.
all proton needs to do is set the game to a reasonible fps, and rename the executable to be excellent. I actually was expecting far more problems.

the only "real" issue i've got is the video clips are notably distorting, like bad video streaming. that *COULD* be that i'm on a usb2.0 harddrive, but you'd think if it can run most games, it can handle a video stream. 
the video is not "unwatchable", just movement distortions. e.g the terrain around the bus hitting the skag was largely distorted, but the bus and skag were fine.

notes: I had quite huge troubles with starting the game. At first I had to rename launcher as everybody suggested. That led me to constant black screen with splash image shown. To fix that I had to enable Proton logging, dig in log file and find that Proton's Vulkan drivers can properly start, then install Vulkan drivers and use more recent NVidia drivers (390 vs 340). As a result, I could start to play normally with my friend. Although, mini-map lags and disappears after each location loading (press M to open big map and mini-map will fix itself). And if game runs in full-screen mode it is better not to Alt-Tab, otherwise it will freeze or squeeze the resolution/image.

title: Borderlands Game of the Year Enhanced

notes:
Firstly to get the game to launch you have to rename the launcher, go to the game install folder and delete or rename the old "Launcher.exe" to "Launcher.bk", rename "BorderlandsGOTY.exe" to "Launcher.exe". Next for performance tweaks, in game turn off dynamic shadows they mess with the framerate badly, frame lock to 60FPS, borerless window (720 or 1080), I prefer Vsync off but if you have bad tearing turn it on at performance cost. Config tweaks, you need to locate the game proton folder, usually ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/729040/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/My Games/Borderlands Game of the Year/WillowGame/Config/
Edit "WillowEngine.ini". Change "HasVoiceEnabled=True" to false (for MP lag). Replace "DefaultPostProcessName=WillowEngineMaterials.WillowScenePostProcess" with "DefaultPostProcessName=WillowEngineMaterials.RyanScenePostProcess". Set "MaxParticleResize=0" to 5000 (or higher if you want more particles). Lastly make sure in your Nvidia X server settings panel, powermizer is set to "Prefer performance"
title: Borderlands Game of the Year Enhanced

notes:
Install game - - - 
Rename Launcher.exe to Launcher.exe.bak - - - 
Rename BorderlandsGOTY.exe to Launcher.exe - - - 
Next, using WINETRICKS, add some ingredients to Borderlands soup: 
WINESERVER="/home/<Your_Username>/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Proton 4.11/dist/bin/wineserver" WINEPREFIX="/home/<Your_Username>/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/729040/pfx" winetricks d3dx9 d3dx9_36 vcrun2005 vcrun2008 vcrun2015 - - -
In game menu set frame rate to 'unlimited' - - - 
Multiplayer not tested - - - 
Gameplay very smooth, FPS=120-144 on MEDIUM, 90-125 on HIGH preset
title: Borderlands Game of the Year Enhanced

notes: Works perfectly for me (Fedora Cinnamon, 2700x, Vega 56) after renaming Launcher.exe to Launcher.exe.old and Borderlands.exe to Launcher.exe. To avoid stuttering, switch frame rate cap from "22-62 smoothed" to 75 fps or higher. On my system, the "smoothing" made the game play LESS smoothly. Also, the game does not automatically go full-screen on launch. I have to start the game and then click on icon in Cinnamon panel to go full-screen (you will hear the audio from the logo animations). On the positive side, I am able to alt-tab in and out of the game without trouble, which I can't do in BL2 (HD-enhanced version) without exiting completely.
title: Borderlands Game of the Year Enhanced

notes:
Works great after applying the following launch option recommended by another user on here: DO=(%command%); "${DO[@]/%Launcher.exe/BorderlandsGOTY.exe}"

Getting 110-150 fps on 1440p with everything maxed out except I disabled Depth of Field and Antialiasing.
title: Borderlands Game of the Year Enhanced

notes:
Works perfectly after setting the launch option DO=(%command%); "${DO[@]/%Launcher.exe/BorderlandsGOTY.exe}". I seem to get ~58FPS (not sure if vsync is enabled, but I always seem to get this FPS)

Have played for ~8 hours with no crashing, and Alt-Tab out of the game works perfectly.

Was able to import my character from regular Borderlands by copying it from the Borderlands wine prefix to the same path in the Borderlands Enhanced prefix.
title: Borderlands Game of the Year Enhanced

notes: Changing lunching options didn't help but after renaming BorderlandsGOTY.exe to Launcher.exe the game started. I experienced flickering once but it stopped. Works great on 3440x1440.
title: Borderlands Game of the Year Enhanced

notes:
Didn't need to set any Launch optons in properties. I followed SpecificKing's post in this reddit thread: https://old.reddit.com/r/SteamPlay/comments/b91sfi/anyone_tried_borderlands_remaster_yet/
I will try to quote that post below  (edited in dashes for line separators, not sure how it will look here):
-------------------------------------- Beginning of quote ------------------------------
Open the terminal and ctrl + shift + v this, replace YOURUSERNAME with your current username:
--------------------- 
cd /home/YOURUSERNAME/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/729040/
--------------------- 
Once you see the terminal is pointed at that directory ctrl + shift + v this into the same terminal:
--------------------- 
WINEPREFIX=$PWD/pfx winetricks d3dx9 d3dx9_36 vcrun2005 vcrun2008 vcrun2015
--------------------- 
Once those finish installing, this should take a couple minutes, paste in this command:
--------------------- 
WINEPREFIX=$PWD/pfx winetricks glsl=disabled
--------------------- 
Now paste in this (again ctrl + shift + v, not ctrl + v):
--------------------- 
WINEPREFIX=$PWD/pfx winecfg
--------------------- 
The windows version at the bottom should be windows 7.
--------------------- 
In the graphics section unselect the option for the window manager to decorate windows. Then select emulate virtual desktop and put in your native desktop resolution in the two boxes (1920 x 1080 for example.) Hit apply.
--------------------- 
Optional:
--------------------- 
On the desktop integration tab, under the item box click the dropdown and select desktop. Choose black as the color, it's more pleasing to the eyes imo. Hit OK. Should work fine after that.
-------------------------------------- End of quote ------------------------------
In-game options, I turned off vSync and set frame limiter to unlimited.
title: Borderlands Game of the Year Enhanced

notes:
Was able to launch after make the changes other people have suggested.   Game crashes WAY too often.  Multiplayer works but I get a hard crash to where I have to do a hardrestart of my PC.  
I have turned down settings to medium and Unlimited FPS and still running into issues. It is almost so bad that I am not sure I want to play any more with my friend. My FPS counter says I am getting about 65fps right now. but for somereason it keeps crashing on me when I go to new areas or for no reason.
title: Borderlands Game of the Year Enhanced

notes: Good performance. The game launcher crashes. A quick fix is to navigate to the install folder, inside "/Binaries/Win64/" and rename BorderlandsGOTY.exe to Launcher.exe. Simply hit play and the game will launch.
title: Borderlands Game of the Year Enhanced

notes: Need to rename BorderlandsGOTY.exe to Launcher.exe, game crashes sometimes. After exiting it won't start unless system reboot.

notes: The game works perfectly after setting the launch options with "DO=(%command%); "${DO[@]/%Launcher.exe/BorderlandsGOTY.exe}" Got 4K 60FPS ultra settings on my setup, with perfect graphics, sound and controls.
